channel.title: Ransomware on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Ransomware on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: security: GigaWiper: Anatomy of a destructive backdoor assembled from multiple malware
item.description:
Microsoft describes GigaWiper as a destructive backdoor that merges wiping and ransomware-like capabilities into one platform. The analysis focuses on how it reuses code from multiple malware families and offers guidance for detection and defense.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: One intrusion, two cyberattackers: Uncovering parallel threat activity
item.description:
Microsoft describes a ransomware case where two threat actors operated in parallel during the same intrusion, using overlapping tactics and evasion methods. The report emphasizes that isolated telemetry can miss complex attacks when activity from multiple actors is blended together.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: The Gentlemen ransomware: Dissecting a self-propagating Go encryptor
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence analyzed The Gentlemen, a Go-based ransomware used by Storm-2697 affiliates. It uses per-file ephemeral key encryption and a self-propagation module to spread across networks through simultaneous lateral movement techniques.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Exposing Fox Tempest: A malware-signing service operation
item.description:
Microsoft says Fox Tempest is a financially motivated threat actor running a malware-signing-as-a-service operation used by other cybercriminals to help distribute malicious code, including ransomware. The service has been linked to groups such as Vanilla Tempest and Storm.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog
